channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: St. Louis Blues fire Drew Bannister and hire Jim Montgomery as coach
item.description: The St. Louis Blues have fired coach Drew Bannister and hired Jim Montgomery as his replacement just five days after the 2022 Jack Adams Award winner was let go by the Boston Bruins. Blues president and general manager Doug Armstrong announced the stunning change Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Appalachian State fans pelt James Madison players with snowballs before game
item.description: As the James Madison Dukes were entering the field from the tunnel, Appalachian State Mountaineer fans bombarded the players with snowballs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russell and Hamilton give Mercedes a 1-2 finish in Las Vegas
item.description: George Russell won the Formula 1 race Saturday night in the Las Vegas Grand Prix and his Mercedes teammate Lewis Hamilton was second.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utes out of bowl game contention after 31-28 loss
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Down to their fifth-string quarterback, the Utes football team almost pulled out a spectacular comeback against #22 Iowa State, only to lose its seventh straight game, 31-28.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets look for coach, GM amid chaos
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – With the Jets’ season completely off the rails, it wasn't a big surprise when General Manager Joe Douglas was fired this week. Changes need to be made, and Douglas was carrying around a 30-64 record over five-plus seasons.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Barbra Banda’s goal leads Orlando over Washington 1-0 for NWSL championship. Marta earns title
item.description: Barbra Banda scored in the 37th minute and the Orlando Pride beat the Washington Spirit 1-0 to win their first National Women’s Soccer League championship.
item.dc:creator: Avery Osen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Penguins' Crosby scores 600th NHL goal
item.description: Sidney Crosby became the 21st player in NHL history to score 600 goals when he reached the mark in the second period against the Utah Hockey Club on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dan Scifo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After fans storm field early, No. 21 Arizona St stops Hail Mary try for 28-23 win over No. 14 BYU
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Cam Skattebo was the workhorse once again for Arizona State, carrying the Sun Devils on his back by rushing for 147 yards and three touchdowns. His reward was a well-earned celebration with fans. Twice. Skattebo had another big game, Sam Leavitt threw for 247 yards and No.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Verstappen captures 4th F1 championship after Mercedes sweep of Las Vegas Grand Prix
item.description: Max Verstappen cruised to a fourth consecutive Formula 1 championship Saturday night by finishing fifth in the Las Vegas Grand Prix.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge says ex-coach was within 'protected speech' in opposing trans athlete's inclusion in girls competition
item.description: During his fight to get his coaching job back, John Parks was told by a federal judge he was within his free speech rights when voicing a stance on transgender athletes in girls sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Argentina's Racing wins its first Copa Sudamericana championship by beating Brazil's Cruzeiro 3-1
item.description: Gaston Martirena and Adrian Martinez scored first-half goals as Argentina's Racing won its first Copa Sudamericana championship by beating Brazil's Cruzeiro 3-1 in the final on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cam Ward breaks Bernie Kosar's single-season Miami passing yards and completions records
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Another week, another pair of records for Miami quarterback Cam Ward — breaking 40-year-old marks by Bernie Kosar in both cases.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Formula 1 expected to expand grid to 11 teams as early as next week for Cadillac to enter in 2026
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Formula 1 is expected to approve grid expansion for an 11th team originally started by Michael Andretti as early as next week, The Associated Press has learned. The team will be called Cadillac F1 and powered by Ferrari engines when it enters the sport in 2026.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU women's soccer "feeling pretty good" ahead of NCAA tournament
item.description: Colorado Mesa women’s soccer is in. The Mavs received an at-large bid into the Division II NCAA tournament.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Police may have suspects in Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce burglaries
item.description: One week after high end burglaries targeting Kansas City Chiefs stars Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce, FOX4 is learning who could be behind them.
item.dc:creator: Malik Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Killorn helps the Ducks beat the Blackhawks 3-2 for their third straight win
item.description: Alex Killorn had a goal and an assist in the third period, and the Anaheim Ducks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Tuesday night for their season-high third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Jets are a dumpster fire
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The Jets are a remarkable dumpster fire. Head coach Robert Saleh fired. GM Joe Douglas fired. Offensive coordinator Nathaniel Hackett demoted. They are a 3 and 8 football team and will most certainly miss the playoffs for the 14th consecutive year.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Bulls great Bob Love dead at 81 after battle with cancer
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — An all-star for the Chicago Bulls in the 1970's, franchise great Bob Love has died at the age of 81 after a long battle with cancer, the team announced Monday. Love played from 1968 to 1976 with the Chicago Bulls and was affectionately nicknamed "Butterbean" for his kind demeanor.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: BYU still controls own destiny for Big 12 Championship
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU is in a position it hasn't been in all season long -- trying to bounce back from a loss. The Cougars suffered their first defeat of the season Saturday night, a disappointing 17-13 loss to Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: FAU fires Tom Herman as coach after a 6-16 record in parts of two seasons
item.description: Florida Atlantic fired coach Tom Herman on Monday with two games left in his second season at the school, neither of which resulted in a winning record.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz comeback falls short against Clippers, 116-105
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - James Harden moved into second place on the NBA's career 3-point list and had 20 points and 11 assists to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Utah Jazz 116-105 on Sunday night to end a three-game losing streak. Harden moved past Ray Allen with No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
